title: Sort out the mess
prompt: names.txt is an unsorted list full of duplicates. Produce sorted.txt: the same names, alphabetical, each appearing once. sort FILE sorts lines; sort -u sorts AND drops duplicates; > saves the result.

files.contents:
charlie
alice
bob
alice
dave
bob
charlie
eve
alice

details.restate: Create sorted.txt containing the five distinct names from names.txt in alphabetical order — alice, bob, charlie, dave, eve — one per line, no duplicates.

io.because: -u means unique: sort the lines and keep each one once

help.plain: One sort flag does both jobs — alphabetize and de-duplicate — then redirect the result into sorted.txt.
help.analogy: Alphabetizing a stack of business cards and tossing the doubles in the same pass.
help.nudge: sort has a flag that removes repeated lines while it sorts. Then where does > come in?
